problem_id: Two-Way Interoperability between the Single Window System and Department Systems
team_name: SyncBridge Karnataka: Event-Driven Bidirectional Interoperability Mesh with Zero-Touch System Integration
summary: Design a layer that propagates service requests both ways between Karnataka's Single Window System and the legacy department systems, using the Unique Business Identifier (UBID) as the common join key.
key_steps: Use UBID as the universal join key
Establish direction 1: SWS to Department Propagation
Establish direction 2: Department to SWS Propagation
optimization_claims: Eliminating split-brain means businesses update once, not forty times
Revenue & Compliance Impact: Officer productivity rises, citizen grievances drop, and compliance timelines shrink from weeks to hours
constraints_addressed: SWS and legacy departments operate as independent authorities with no shared state
A business can file the same request in two places simultaneously, creating divergent records
Department schemas, identifiers, validation rules, and APIs are heterogeneous and frozen
Some departments emit no events at all
missing_components: Performance optimization for handling large-scale data
Real-time conflict resolution for critical updates
Long-term scalability plan for adding more departments and services
